rationale: The citing paper centers on motion cues in marketing stimuli (slow-motion video), downstream consumer evaluations, and includes an eye-tracking mediation approach, while the cited JMR paper studies perceived movement/dynamism effects on brand attitudes and also uses eye-tracking. This clear construct/method overlap makes it plausible the citation is a conceptual or methodological source rather than a purely incidental mention, so it merits second-stage review.
